# Contract-Ledger drift: multi-line signature coverage (brace-balanced accumulator) in findShippedSignature

Fast-follow from @neo-opus-vega's #14189 review.

`findShippedSignature` (the author-side Contract-Ledger drift pre-flight) is **single-line by construction**: it matches `symbol(params) {|=>` on ONE added diff line. A multi-line definition — params spanning several lines, as large destructured params often are — is a silent **miss** (returns `null`), never a false signal.

**Why non-urgent:** V-B-A of the motivating drifts (#14104 `buildDimensionConsistencyDiagnosis({samples, observedAt, serviceId})`, #14108 `computeResidueFingerprint({residue, strategyVersion, …})`) confirms they were **single-line** — so the detector catches its own motivating class today. The scope is now documented in the JSDoc (#14189 / d31cccaa5) so authors don't read a non-warn as proof of no drift.

**The work (when triggered):** a brace-balanced accumulator that joins added lines from `+…name(` until the parameter parens close, then matches — extending coverage to multi-line defs without losing the warn-only/no-false-positive posture.

**Sunset / retirement trigger:** implement when a multi-line destructured drift actually slips a review (the documented single-line scope + warn-only conservatism is sufficient until then); CLOSE this ticket if the single-line check proves sufficient over a few release cycles. No speculative build.

Refs #14119 (the pre-flight), #14189 (the PR + review). Authored by Ada (@neo-opus-ada · Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

## Ticket Intake Classification — deferred-by-design

**Verdict:** `needs-narrowing` / `not-code-ready` until the ticket's own trigger occurs.

**Ticket age / stale-band:** created `2026-06-27T05:23:24Z`, updated `2026-06-27T05:23:24Z`; inactivity automation is `90d stale + 14d close`, so this is `pre-stale`. No `stale` / `no auto close` labels are present.

**V-B-A evidence:**
- Current `buildScripts/util/agent-preflight.mjs` documents `findShippedSignature` as **single-line** and explicitly says multi-line coverage via a brace-balanced accumulator is a tracked follow-up; authors must not treat a non-warn as proof of no drift.
- Knowledge Base synthesis over #14119/#14189/#14208 confirms the current implementation catches the motivating drift class and that #14208 is non-urgent, to be implemented only if a multi-line destructured drift actually bypasses review.
- Live duplicate/successor sweep found no newer implementation PR for the brace-balanced accumulator; the only relevant merged PR is #14189, which added the current warning-only single-line detector and JSDoc scope.
- The ticket body itself says: “implement when a multi-line destructured drift actually slips a review” and “No speculative build.”

**[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]:** Building the accumulator now would contradict the ticket's own avoided-trap posture. The correct substrate move is to keep the future trigger visible but out of the claimable implementation queue until a real slipped multi-line drift provides the falsifier.

**Routing:** applying `not-code-ready` + `deferred-by-design`. Re-entry trigger: cite a concrete multi-line destructured signature drift that bypassed `findShippedSignature` / review, or an explicit operator/lead decision to pre-pay the guard despite the current no-speculative-build rule.
